meaning: adj.不适当的,无能的,不称职的
examples: Yes, you are often clumsy, even inept.
Perhaps they should add inept parents to the list.
He wanted to hold hands. I was young, inept and scared.
On the other hand, a corrupt or inept consultant can cost a pension billions.
And it is, it is just gloriously inept chaos that is beautifully captured on film.
